findings.threshold: 99.9%+ availability via HA DAPR control plane + multi-replica deployment
findings.description: Production SLO cannot be asserted by in-repo unit/integration tests. Validated indirectly via four DAPR building-block health checks (sidecar, state store, pub/sub, config store) wired centrally in HealthCheckBuilderExtensions.AddEventStoreDaprHealthChecks, 3s per-check timeout, 'ready' probe tag for K8s, plus chaos tests proving recovery. HA topology/multi-replica deployment is a runtime (Helm/K8s) concern not covered here.
evidence: D:/Hexalith.EventStore/src/Hexalith.EventStore/HealthChecks/HealthCheckBuilderExtensions.cs — registers dapr-sidecar (Unhealthy), dapr-statestore (Unhealthy), dapr-pubsub (Degraded), dapr-configstore (Degraded) with 3s timeout and 'ready' tag
D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.IntegrationTests/ContractTests/ChaosResilienceTests.cs — Redis stop/start cycle confirms recovery to healthy state
recommendations: Add runtime SLO monitoring (OTLP → Prometheus SLO alerts) to tie synthetic probes to SLA.
Document HA K8s topology (2+ replicas, PDB, anti-affinity) as a deployment runbook.

findings.threshold: No event loss under any failure scenario (state store crash, actor crash, pub/sub down, partition)
findings.description: Persist-then-publish state machine (ADR-P2) guarantees events land in state store before publish is attempted; failure to publish stores an UnpublishedEventsRecord and registers a drain reminder — events are never dropped. Tests explicitly assert 'events still in state store' after publish failure, and chaos tests restart Redis mid-flight with subsequent commands completing.
evidence: D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Events/AtLeastOnceDeliveryTests.cs — PublishEventsAsync_TotalFailure_EventsSafeInStateStore (line 126) asserts events remain safe when pub/sub unavailable; AggregateActor_PublishFailed_EventsNotLostInStateStore (line 153) asserts SaveStateAsync invoked before publish

D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Events/PersistThenPublishResilienceTests.cs — ProcessCommand_PublishFailed_EventsStillInStateStore (line 317) asserts events: key persisted even when publish fails

findings.threshold: Deterministic replay from last checkpoint after state-store recovery — no manual steps
findings.description: ActorStateMachine writes an 8-stage PipelineState checkpoint keyed per correlationId. On rehydration the actor loads the pipeline checkpoint and resumes from the last persisted stage (e.g., EventsStored → Publish retry). Chaos test StateStoreRestart_ExistingAggregate_RehydratesCorrectly confirms snapshot + tail events reload and a third command progresses to Completed. No manual intervention required.
evidence: D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Actors/ActorStateMachineTests.cs — Checkpoint/LoadPipelineState/Cleanup/Overwrite tests (covers Processing, EventsStored transitions; state machine enforces key convention {tenant}:{domain}:{aggId}:pipeline:{corrId})

D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Events/PersistThenPublishResilienceTests.cs — ResumeFromEventsStored_PublishFails_UnpublishedRecordStored (line 368) proves pipeline resumes at EventsStored after simulated crash
recommendations: ActorStateMachineTests explicitly exercises Processing and EventsStored stages only; extend unit coverage to enumerate all 8 CommandStatus values (Processing, EventsStored, Published, PublishFailed, and the 4 terminal states) for auditable completeness.

findings.threshold: All events persisted during pub/sub outage delivered post-recovery via DAPR retry policies
findings.description: When publish fails, the actor checkpoints an UnpublishedEventsRecord (correlationId, start/end sequence, retry count, reason) and registers a drain reminder using EventDrainOptions with MaxDrainPeriod clamping and boundary handling. Drain retries via DAPR sidecar retry/circuit-breaker policies. ResiliencyConfigurationTests verifies the DAPR-native policy wiring (per rule #4: no custom retry logic).
evidence: D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Events/AtLeastOnceDeliveryTests.cs — partial failure, total failure, circuit breaker fast-fail all transition to PublishFailed without event loss

D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Events/PersistThenPublishResilienceTests.cs — DrainReminderRegistered, DrainReminderUsesConfiguredTiming, DrainReminderClampsToMaxPeriod, boundary-value theories for InitialDrainDelay/DrainPeriod

findings.category: No Duplicate Persistence on Actor Crash
findings.threshold: Actor crash between persist and publish → no duplicate persistence (checkpointed state machine)
findings.description: IdempotencyRecord is checked on actor entry; resuming from a PipelineState in EventsStored skips re-invoking the domain and re-persisting events, instead loading existing events from state store and retrying only the publish step. ResumeFromEventsStored_PrepareFails_MetadataUnavailable explicitly throws rather than silently re-persisting, proving the state machine refuses to double-write. Write-once event keys (rule #11) provide defense in depth.
evidence: D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Events/PersistThenPublishResilienceTests.cs — ResumeFromEventsStored_PublishFails_UnpublishedRecordStored (line 368), ResumeFromEventsStored_PublishFails_MetadataMissingAfterLoad_StillStoresRecordFromLoadedEvents (line 474), ResumeFromEventsStored_PrepareFails_MetadataUnavailable_ThrowsInsteadOfSilentPublishFailed (line 529) — proves resume path loads existing events from state store and does not re-invoke domain

findings.threshold: Optimistic concurrency conflicts never silently overwrite; client receives HTTP 409 Conflict
findings.description: ETag-based concurrency on the aggregate metadata key rejects stale-ETag writes (MetadataKey_StaleEtagUpdate_IsRejected). The ConcurrencyConflictExceptionHandler maps the exception to 409 ProblemDetails with correlationId, and UX-DR10 hardening suppresses aggregateId/tenantId/conflictSource leak in the 409 body.
evidence: D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Actors/ActorConcurrencyConflictTests.cs — MetadataKey_StaleEtagUpdate_IsRejected (line 107) asserts stale-ETag second write returns non-success at the state-store boundary

D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Server.Tests/Commands/ConcurrencyConflictExceptionHandlerTests.cs — TryHandleAsync_ConcurrencyConflictException_Returns409ProblemDetails (line 36) asserts StatusCode 409, Title 'Conflict', Type ProblemTypeUris.ConcurrencyConflict; additional tests assert UX-DR10 information-leak suppression

findings.threshold: Defined Recovery Time Objective and Recovery Point Objective
findings.description: GAP-14 in architecture.md explicitly defers DR SLOs to v2/v3 operational readiness. No RTO/RPO targets are specified in PRD/architecture. No test validates recovery-time bounds. This is a GA blocker if the product is sold with availability/DR commitments.
evidence: No test file in D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests references RTO, RPO, or disaster-recovery timing assertions (grep for backup|restore|failover|disaster returned only the Admin.Cli *tenant event archive* feature, not infrastructure DR)
recommendations: Define RTO/RPO targets (e.g., RTO 15 min, RPO 5 min) for v1 GA or document the deferred-to-v2 SLA carve-out in the customer-facing SLA.
Add a load-bearing DR runbook and measure actual recovery time in a chaos test that exercises regional failover.

findings.threshold: Multi-region/multi-AZ failover exercised; control-plane HA verified under node loss
findings.description: No failover test exists. ChaosResilienceTests covers single-instance Redis stop/start (recovery from outage in the same cluster), not topology-level failover. GAP-14 confirms mechanism is undefined. 99.9% SLO is implied but unvalidated.
evidence: D:/Hexalith.EventStore/tests/Hexalith.EventStore.IntegrationTests/ContractTests/ChaosResilienceTests.cs — scope is single-node Redis lifecycle, not regional/AZ failover
No test file matches failover|multi-region|multi-az in tests/
recommendations: Document the intended failover topology (DAPR control-plane HA, state-store replication strategy: Redis Sentinel/Cluster, CosmosDB multi-region, etc.).
Add a Tier-3 chaos test that kills a DAPR control-plane node (placement/actor service) and confirms actor reassignment completes within target RTO.

findings.category: Disaster Recovery — Backup Immutability & Restore Integrity
findings.threshold: Infrastructure backups are immutable, restore succeeds with byte-identical data
findings.description: No infrastructure backup/restore tests exist. The Admin.Cli 'Backup' commands (BackupCommandTests, DaprBackupCommandServiceTests, BackupsPageTests) are TENANT-LEVEL event-archive operations for auditability/export, not disaster-recovery of the underlying state store. Backup immutability and restore integrity are explicitly deferred (GAP-14).
evidence: No tests assert WORM/object-lock, encryption-at-rest of backups, or restore-integrity hash comparisons
recommendations: Add a backup policy definition (frequency, retention, immutability mode: S3 Object Lock / Azure immutable blobs).
Add a restore-integrity test: snapshot event log → restore to fresh cluster → replay → assert hash of events matches source.

findings.threshold: OpenTelemetry → OTLP collector validated in Aspire + Jaeger + Grafana/Tempo
findings.description: Aspire OTLP wiring is validated via OpenTelemetryRegistrationTests and the Observability test folder. Jaeger/Grafana/Tempo targets are configured but not exercised in CI, so drift between config and reality is not detected automatically. Traceability report flags this PARTIAL.
recommendations: Add a CI job that spins up Jaeger/Tempo containers, submits a command, and asserts at least one span lands in the collector.

dr_gap_assessment.note: GAP-14 explicitly defers DR to v2/v3 operational readiness. Admin.Cli 'backup' features are tenant event-archive operations, not infrastructure DR. No RTO/RPO targets, no failover tests, no backup immutability/restore-integrity tests. If v1 GA is sold with an availability or DR commitment, this is a BLOCKER; otherwise it is a documented v2 deferral.

ci_flags.tier3_continue_on_error: true — .github/workflows/ci.yml line 322 sets continue-on-error: true on the aspire-tests job (Tier 3 Aspire Contract Tests, which includes ChaosResilienceTests)
ci_flags.impact: A chaos-test regression (e.g., post-Redis-restart rehydration silently fails) will NOT break the build nor block merge to main. Reliability invariants NFR22-NFR25 are protected by Tier 2 unit tests (required), but the end-to-end proof under real Aspire/Docker/Redis is advisory-only. This is acceptable for flaky-infra tolerance during bring-up but should be revisited before GA.
ci_flags.additional_notes: Tier 1 + Tier 2 (build-and-test job) are NOT marked continue-on-error — core reliability unit and integration tests do block the build.

priority_actions: P0 — Define RTO/RPO for v1 GA or explicitly document the DR carve-out in the customer SLA (GAP-14 unblocker).
P0 — Add backup immutability policy (WORM / object-lock) and a restore-integrity test for the state store.
P1 — Add a DAPR control-plane failover chaos test (kill placement service, assert actor reassignment within target RTO).
P1 — Remove continue-on-error: true from the aspire-tests CI job, or gate on a required subset of chaos tests, before GA so reliability regressions fail the build.
P2 — Extend ActorStateMachineTests to explicitly enumerate all 8 CommandStatus stages (4 non-terminal + 4 terminal) for audit completeness.
P2 — Add a CI job that runs Jaeger/Tempo containers and asserts OTLP spans are received (close NFR31 PARTIAL gap).
summary: Reliability is architecturally strong: zero-event-loss (NFR22), deterministic replay (NFR23), at-least-once delivery (NFR24), no-duplicate-persist on crash (NFR25), and 409 on concurrency (NFR26) are all PASS with concrete unit + integration + chaos evidence. The GA risk concentrates in disaster recovery — GAP-14 defers RTO/RPO, failover, and backup immutability with no compensating tests; combined with Tier-3 chaos tests running continue-on-error in CI, operational resilience is under-tested at the topology layer. Recommend gating GA on defining RTO/RPO, adding a restore-integrity test, and hardening the CI reliability signal.
